title: Censoring-aware AFT sidecar for remaining laps to next stop
summary: Rows near race end are naturally right-censored if no further pit stop occurs, which standard binary framing discards.
rationale: Rows near race end are naturally right-censored if no further pit stop occurs, which standard binary framing discards. XGBoost’s AFT survival objective explicitly supports uncensored and right-censored labels, making it a clean way to predict remaining laps to next pit and then derive a next-lap stop probability or an expected-time feature.
implementation_hint: Build labels as `time_to_next_pit` for rows with a future stop and right-censor rows that finish without another stop. Train `objective=survival:aft` on current features, then add OOF expected remaining laps / transformed 1-lap hazard as sidecar features or stack input.
expected_effect: Can capture longer-horizon structure better than a one-step label alone, especially for cars clearly destined to stop later but not immediately.
risk: AFT optimizes survival likelihood, not AUC directly, so gains may only appear after stacking; label construction must be strictly causal and race-bounded.
